heaviest_rainfall.status: The heaviest total rainfall occurred on 09 November 2025, dropping 47.89mm of rain over the 144.8 hour storm duration with a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: On 02 September 2025, the rain fell at an exceptional rate, peaking at 34.3mm per hour, the heaviest downpour recorded.  A total of 21.19mm fell over the 15.0 hour storm.

longest_rain.status: The longest rain storm on record lasted 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm of rain fell and at one point was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The highest recorded number of lightning strikes in a single day is {highest_count} on {highest_date} since records began on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: A storm is in the area.  This is the first such event since 21 December 2025.

top_10_dry.status: This period, starting 23 December 2025, is ranked 7th among the longest dry periods since 23 September 2024.  It has lasted 9 days so far.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 26 November 2025 and lasted for 14 days.

cold_snaps.status: At the moment, it is not a cold snap.  This year has seen 6 cold snaps; typically, 3.7 cold snaps happen every year.

extreme.status: The highest recorded temperature difference is 26.7°C from 29 April 2025 (when the temperature reached 27.1°C but fell to 0.4°C).

heatwaves.status: At the moment, it is not a heatwaves.  This year has seen 6 heatwavess; typically, 1.9 heatwavess happen every year.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was recorded on 18 July 2025.  The total number of occurrences since 1973 number is only 62.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (197°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The largest wind gust ever was on 13 October 2012 with a value of 93.7 mph.

run.status: The record wind run remains at 1737 miles from 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The highest wind speed ever seen is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the inside temperature (down 8.3°C), outside temperature (down 5.1°C), and feels like temperature (down 4.3°C).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 285 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in dew point temperature (up 9.0°C), outside temperature (up 9.1°C), cloud levels (up 321 m), and inside humidity (up 7%) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 284.7m lower than yesterday, 321.3m lower than the past week, 33.7m higher than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 828m to a maximum of 2280m, averaging 996.2m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 1317.5m and 962.5m respectively.

summary.summary: Inside; temperatures should be quite comfortable. Outside; icy, freezing temperatures. Also, the atmosphere will feel parched and dry and calm and still conditions are expected. Chance of light rain, clouds will occasionally block sunlight, and moderate levels of sunlight will be present. Stable conditions are expected under a high pressure system.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (-5.7°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
